meta.title: DIAS Template – Dental Lab Workflow Dataset
meta.source: STC LAB DIAS Format (UG, Fellowship & PG)
meta.description: Each procedure lists sequential lab steps. Every step carries a next_steps array of step IDs that follow it in the lab workflow.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Gropers
steps.description: Take impressions of the upper/lower arch and fabricate the Groper appliance using stainless-steel wire. Verify wire adaptation and acrylic coverage on the model before advancing to the space maintainer step.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Space Maintainer
steps.description: Fit the fabricated space maintainer on the model. Verify band adaptation and loop position over the edentulous space. Polish all wire ends smooth. This is the final appliance — check passive fit before delivery to the clinic.

steps.name: Splint Fabrication
steps.description: Thermoform or press-cure hard acrylic over the arch model. Trim occlusal surface flat, ensure even posterior contacts, and polish all edges. Verify fit on the model before delivery.

steps.name: Splint Fabrication
steps.description: Thermoform soft vinyl/ethylene-vinyl acetate sheet over the arch model under vacuum. Trim 1 mm below the gingival margin, smooth all edges, and verify comfortable seating before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour a secondary working cast using implant analogs seated in the impression. Allow complete setting of type IV dental stone. Trim and verify analog positions before proceeding to jig or wax-up fabrication.

steps.name: Verification Jig
steps.description: Fabricate a custom acrylic verification jig connecting all implant analogs on the secondary cast. Sectionalize and rejoin to eliminate model distortion. Send to the clinic for passive fit verification at implant level before any prosthetic fabrication.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Fabricate the crown as specified for this work type. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusion and proximal contacts, finish and polish before delivery.

steps.name: Ceramic Coping
steps.description: Fabricate or mill the zirconia coping on the die. Check marginal fit and minimum ceramic thickness (0.5 mm coping, 1.5 mm total). Sandblast and prepare the surface before hand-layering ceramic porcelain.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the multi-unit FPD wax pattern on the dies. Verify connector dimensions (minimum 4×3 mm cross-section for posterior), pontic tissue contact, and marginal adaptation on all abutment dies before investing for casting.

steps.name: Metal Coping
steps.description: Cast the metal coping, verify marginal fit on the die, and prepare the metal surface (sandblast, oxidise, apply opaque) for ceramic bonding. Ceramic build-up proceeds after the coping is approved by the doctor.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the multi-unit FPD wax pattern on the dies. Verify connector dimensions (minimum 4×3 mm cross-section for posterior), pontic tissue contact, and marginal adaptation on all abutment dies before investing for casting.

steps.name: Ceramic Facing
steps.description: Apply ceramic only to the facial surface of the metal coping — lingual and occlusal surfaces remain metal. Bake to bisque stage and send to clinic for try-in before final glaze firing.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Implant Wax Pattern
steps.description: Wax up the castable abutment over the implant component on the analog. Ensure correct emergence profile, axial wall height, and anti-rotation features before investing for casting.

steps.name: Metal Cast Custom Abutment
steps.description: Cast the waxed abutment in the prescribed alloy and verify fit on the analog. Polish the supra-gingival portion and prepare the surface for ceramic bonding. Proceed to ceramic application after abutment try-in clearance from the doctor.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Package the prepared die/model with complete shade prescription (body shade, stump shade, gingival characterisation) and send to the outsource lab for E-max gingival porcelain pressing. Verify receipt and inspect on return before delivery.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Send the prepared die and shade guide details to the outsource lab for full-contour or cut-back E-max crown fabrication. Include any special characterisation requests and the patient photograph if available.

steps.name: E-max Veneer Outsource
steps.description: Dispatch the prepped die model with shade, stump shade, incisal halo notes, and patient photographs to the outsource lab for E-max veneer pressing. Include the diagnostic wax-up if one was completed.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the Maryland bridge wax pattern, shaping the retainer wings to follow tooth contour with adequate thickness (≥0.5 mm) on the enamel surface. Ensure the pontic maintains proper tissue contact form before sending for casting.

steps.name: Maryland Bridge – Retainer
steps.description: Cast the metal retainer wing and verify precise enamel-side fit on the abutment die. Wing should be rigid yet minimally invasive. Polish the fitting surface to improve adhesive bonding before adding the pontic.

steps.name: Maryland Bridge – Pontic
steps.description: Attach the ceramic or metal pontic to the cast retainer wing. Verify tissue-contact convexity, embrasure form, and pontic width. Build ceramic and send for bisque try-in before final glazing and delivery.

steps.name: Bisque Trial (Layering)
steps.description: Complete the 'Bisque Trial (Layering)' step as per the lab prescription for MARYLAND BRIDGE (MARYLAND BRIDGE). Verify fit, contours, and finish on the working cast before proceeding to the next step.

steps.name: Welding / Soldering
steps.description: Join the sectioned metal components using laser welding or conventional solder on the indexed working cast. Check joint integrity under magnification. Re-verify passive fit on the cast and adjust before polishing and delivery.

steps.name: Primary Coping
steps.description: Wax and cast the inner (primary) coping with strictly parallel walls on the die. Polish external surfaces to a high mirror luster — the sliding fit of the secondary coping depends entirely on this precision.

steps.name: Secondary Coping
steps.description: Fabricate the secondary (outer) coping to telescope precisely over the polished primary coping with a smooth sliding retention. Verify the telescopic fit on the model before ceramic or resin veneering. This is the final restoration.

steps.name: PSI Finishing & Polishing
steps.description: Finish and polish the Patient-Specific Implant (PSI) component to the prescribed surface specification. Mark and verify the screw-access channel alignment. Pack in the sterile kit for delivery to the clinic.

steps.name: Cast Pouring (Secondary Cast)
steps.description: Pour a type IV dental stone secondary working cast from the final impression. Allow complete setting before trimming. Mark arch boundaries and survey before proceeding to block-out or designing.

steps.name: CPD Designing
steps.description: Scan the working cast and design the CPD framework in CAD software. Review rest seat positions, connector width, clasp paths, and saddle extensions with the doctor's prescription before sending the file for printing/milling.

steps.name: DMLS Printing
steps.description: 3D-print the metal framework (Co-Cr or Titanium) via Direct Metal Laser Sintering. Remove supports, sandblast, and verify fit on the working cast. Adjust rest seats and clasp tensions before proceeding to teeth setting.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange acrylic teeth on the wax base over the framework in accordance with the articulation. Check occlusion, midline, tooth shade, and mould. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: CPD Processing
steps.description: Flask the approved wax trial denture and process with heat-cured acrylic resin. Deflask carefully, remove flash, and polish to a smooth finish. Verify fit on the cast and check occlusion before final delivery.

steps.name: Cast Pouring (Secondary Cast)
steps.description: Pour a type IV dental stone secondary working cast from the final impression. Allow complete setting before trimming. Mark arch boundaries and survey before proceeding to block-out or designing.

steps.name: CPD Designing
steps.description: Scan the working cast and design the CPD framework in CAD software. Review rest seat positions, connector width, clasp paths, and saddle extensions with the doctor's prescription before sending the file for printing/milling.

steps.name: DMLS Printing
steps.description: 3D-print the metal framework (Co-Cr or Titanium) via Direct Metal Laser Sintering. Remove supports, sandblast, and verify fit on the working cast. Adjust rest seats and clasp tensions before proceeding to teeth setting.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange acrylic teeth on the wax base over the framework in accordance with the articulation. Check occlusion, midline, tooth shade, and mould. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: CPD Processing
steps.description: Flask the approved wax trial denture and process with heat-cured acrylic resin. Deflask carefully, remove flash, and polish to a smooth finish. Verify fit on the cast and check occlusion before final delivery.

steps.name: Cast Pouring (Secondary Cast)
steps.description: Pour a type IV dental stone secondary working cast from the final impression. Allow complete setting before trimming. Mark arch boundaries and survey before proceeding to block-out or designing.

steps.name: Block Out
steps.description: Survey the secondary cast on the dental surveyor to identify the path of insertion. Apply block-out wax in all undercuts below the survey line. Ensure undercut-free cast surface before wax pattern fabrication.

steps.name: CPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the CPD framework wax pattern on the surveyed cast, including all major/minor connectors, occlusal rests, retention clasps, and guide planes. Check thickness and smoothness before investing for casting.

steps.name: CPD Metal Denture Base (RPD Framework)
steps.description: Cast the wax pattern in Co-Cr alloy. Divest, sandblast, and verify framework fit on the working cast. Check rest seat seating, connector rigidity, and clasp deflection before proceeding to articulation.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange acrylic teeth on the wax base over the framework in accordance with the articulation. Check occlusion, midline, tooth shade, and mould. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: CPD Processing
steps.description: Flask the approved wax trial denture and process with heat-cured acrylic resin. Deflask carefully, remove flash, and polish to a smooth finish. Verify fit on the cast and check occlusion before final delivery.

steps.name: Cast Pouring (Secondary Cast)
steps.description: Pour a type IV dental stone secondary working cast from the final impression. Allow complete setting before trimming. Mark arch boundaries and survey before proceeding to block-out or designing.

steps.name: Block Out
steps.description: Survey the secondary cast on the dental surveyor to identify the path of insertion. Apply block-out wax in all undercuts below the survey line. Ensure undercut-free cast surface before wax pattern fabrication.

steps.name: Wax Pattern
steps.description: Fabricate the wax pattern of the flexible denture base on the blocked-out cast, clearly marking saddle areas, clasp locations, and connector paths for thermoplastic injection.

steps.name: Flexible Denture Base (RPD Framework)
steps.description: Flask the wax pattern and inject thermoplastic nylon (Bredent Polyan/Second Edition) under controlled pressure and temperature. Deflask after full cooling and verify framework fit on the cast before teeth setting.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange acrylic teeth on the wax base over the framework in accordance with the articulation. Check occlusion, midline, tooth shade, and mould. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: Flexible Denture Processing
steps.description: Finalise the flexible denture after teeth setting — finish connector and saddle areas using nylon-compatible polishing compounds only. Do NOT use conventional acrylic burs. Verify flexibility and retention before delivery.

steps.name: Cast Pouring (Secondary Cast)
steps.description: Pour a type IV dental stone secondary working cast from the final impression. Allow complete setting before trimming. Mark arch boundaries and survey before proceeding to block-out or designing.

steps.name: Block Out
steps.description: Survey the secondary cast on the dental surveyor to identify the path of insertion. Apply block-out wax in all undercuts below the survey line. Ensure undercut-free cast surface before wax pattern fabrication.

steps.name: Wax Pattern
steps.description: Fabricate the wax pattern of the flexible denture base on the blocked-out cast, clearly marking saddle areas, clasp locations, and connector paths for thermoplastic injection.

steps.name: Flexible Denture Base (RPD Framework)
steps.description: Flask the wax pattern and inject thermoplastic nylon (Bredent Polyan/Second Edition) under controlled pressure and temperature. Deflask after full cooling and verify framework fit on the cast before teeth setting.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange acrylic teeth on the wax base over the framework in accordance with the articulation. Check occlusion, midline, tooth shade, and mould. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: Flexible Denture Processing
steps.description: Finalise the flexible denture after teeth setting — finish connector and saddle areas using nylon-compatible polishing compounds only. Do NOT use conventional acrylic burs. Verify flexibility and retention before delivery.

steps.name: DMLS Printing
steps.description: Print the full-arch Malo implant bar in Co-Cr using DMLS. After support removal and sandblasting, verify passive fit across all implant analog connections simultaneously — no single-point fit accepted.

steps.name: Diagnostic Wax Up – Manual
steps.description: Wax teeth to ideal esthetic and functional contours on the study model manually. This mock-up is transferred to the patient's mouth at the next appointment for esthetic preview and treatment planning confirmation.

steps.name: Diagnostic Wax Up – CADCAM
steps.description: Design the diagnostic wax-up digitally and mill from PMMA. The milled mock-up delivers greater reproducibility than manual wax-up and can be directly transferred to the mouth as a provisional.

steps.name: PMMA CADCAM Milling
steps.description: Mill the temporary crown from a multi-layer (ML) PMMA block to achieve a natural esthetic gradient. Check margins, occlusal contacts, and interproximal contacts before polishing and delivery as a provisional.

steps.name: PMMA CADCAM Milling
steps.description: Mill the temporary crown from a monolayer PMMA block. Polish the occlusal and axial surfaces to a smooth finish. Verify marginal fit on the die before delivery as a short-term provisional.

steps.name: Temporary Acrylic Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Fabricate a temporary crown using auto-polymerising (cold-cure) acrylic over the prepared tooth on the model. Trim flash, adjust occlusion, and polish smooth. Ensure adequate marginal seal and that the crown will not impinge on soft tissue.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Hawley's Appliance
steps.description: Fabricate the Hawley retainer with a labial bow (.7 mm ss-wire) and Adams clasps on first molars (.7 mm). Ensure the acrylic plate covers the palate fully with smooth borders. Verify passive fit on the model before delivery.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Lip Bumper
steps.description: Fabricate the lip bumper using .045" round wire with an anterior acrylic pad. Insert tube ends into buccal tubes on molar bands and verify 2–3 mm clearance from labial tooth surfaces before delivery.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Inclined Plane
steps.description: Construct the inclined acrylic plane at the prescribed angulation to redirect the opposing tooth/teeth. Verify the inclination with a protractor on the model and ensure smooth guiding surface.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Band & Loop
steps.description: Adapt the stainless-steel band to the abutment tooth and solder the wire loop to maintain the edentulous space. Check loop height and position against the opposing arch. Clean all solder joints before delivery.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Begg's Retainer
steps.description: Fabricate the Begg retainer with labial bow and Adams clasps. Verify that the labial bow lies passively against all anterior teeth and that clasps engage undercuts without excessive force.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Bite Plane
steps.description: Fabricate an anterior bite plane that disoccludes all posterior teeth. The inclined surface should be smooth and at the correct angle for guided anterior movement. Add adequate retention clasps on posterior teeth.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Nance Palatal
steps.description: Fabricate the Nance appliance with bilateral molar bands and a palatal acrylic button (4–5 mm from rugae). Solder the connecting wire to the bands and verify button position does not impinge on soft tissue.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Lingual Arch
steps.description: Fabricate the lingual arch with bilateral molar bands and a soldered lingual bar running 1 mm clear of the lingual surfaces. Verify passive fit and arch form on the model before delivery.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Distal Shoe
steps.description: Fabricate the distal shoe appliance to the dimensions derived from the periapical radiograph. The intraosseous blade must extend to guide the erupting first permanent molar. Review radiographic measurements before fabrication.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Transpalatal
steps.description: Fabricate the transpalatal arch connecting bilateral banded molars. The arch should run 3–4 mm clear of the palatal tissue. Check passive fit and torque in both molar bands before delivery.

steps.name: Oral Appliance – Habit Braking
steps.description: Fabricate the habit-breaking appliance (tongue crib or palatal rake) targeting the identified habit. Ensure all spurs or grids are smooth-ended, adequately rigid, and that retention clasps are tight before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Ceramic Facing
steps.description: Apply ceramic only to the facial surface of the metal coping — lingual and occlusal surfaces remain metal. Bake to bisque stage and send to clinic for try-in before final glaze firing.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Fabricate the crown as specified for this work type. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusion and proximal contacts, finish and polish before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Ceramic Coping
steps.description: Fabricate or mill the zirconia coping on the die. Check marginal fit and minimum ceramic thickness (0.5 mm coping, 1.5 mm total). Sandblast and prepare the surface before hand-layering ceramic porcelain.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Endo Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Cast the metal endo crown that engages the pulp chamber walls for retention. Verify internal chamber fit, marginal accuracy, and interproximal contours before polishing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Endo Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Mill or press a monolithic ceramic endo crown. Check internal chamber fit and marginal seal under magnification. Characterize and glaze to shade before delivery.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Fabricate the crown as specified for this work type. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusion and proximal contacts, finish and polish before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Fabricate the crown as specified for this work type. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusion and proximal contacts, finish and polish before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Ceramic Coping
steps.description: Fabricate or mill the zirconia coping on the die. Check marginal fit and minimum ceramic thickness (0.5 mm coping, 1.5 mm total). Sandblast and prepare the surface before hand-layering ceramic porcelain.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the multi-unit FPD wax pattern on the dies. Verify connector dimensions (minimum 4×3 mm cross-section for posterior), pontic tissue contact, and marginal adaptation on all abutment dies before investing for casting.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Cast the all-metal crown from the wax pattern. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusal thickness and proximal contacts. Polish to a high luster before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the multi-unit FPD wax pattern on the dies. Verify connector dimensions (minimum 4×3 mm cross-section for posterior), pontic tissue contact, and marginal adaptation on all abutment dies before investing for casting.

steps.name: Metal Coping
steps.description: Cast the metal coping, verify marginal fit on the die, and prepare the metal surface (sandblast, oxidise, apply opaque) for ceramic bonding. Ceramic build-up proceeds after the coping is approved by the doctor.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FPD Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the multi-unit FPD wax pattern on the dies. Verify connector dimensions (minimum 4×3 mm cross-section for posterior), pontic tissue contact, and marginal adaptation on all abutment dies before investing for casting.

steps.name: Ceramic Facing
steps.description: Apply ceramic only to the facial surface of the metal coping — lingual and occlusal surfaces remain metal. Bake to bisque stage and send to clinic for try-in before final glaze firing.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.

steps.name: Zirconia Milling
steps.description: Mill the zirconia coping from the CAD design. Sinter to final density. Verify fit on the working cast and check coping thickness before applying hand-layered ceramic.

steps.name: Zirconia Milling
steps.description: Mill and sinter the full-contour zirconia crown/FPD. Check marginal fit, proximal contacts, and occlusion on the cast. Stain, glaze, and crystallize before delivery.

steps.name: PMMA Milling
steps.description: Mill the temporary FPD/crown from a multi-layer PMMA block. Check connector dimensions, margins, and occlusion before polishing and delivery as a provisional.

steps.name: PMMA Milling
steps.description: Mill from a monolayer PMMA block, polish, and verify marginal fit on the die. Confirm pontic tissue contact and occlusal clearance before delivery.

steps.name: Metal Milling
steps.description: Mill the metal crown/FPD substructure from a pre-sintered metal block. Check marginal fit and internal surface before ceramic application.

steps.name: Wax Milling
steps.description: Mill the wax pattern for casting using CAD-designed geometry. Check contour accuracy and marginal definition before investing.

steps.name: DMLS Printing
steps.description: Print the full-arch screw-retained implant bar in Co-Cr using DMLS. After post-processing, verify passive fit across all implant connections simultaneously. Perform screw-tightening sequence check before delivery.

steps.name: DMLS Printing
steps.description: Print the RPD metal framework directly via DMLS. Verify framework fit on the working cast, check all rests and connector dimensions, before proceeding to teeth setting and processing.

steps.name: CD Special Tray
steps.description: Fabricate a custom acrylic special tray over the primary cast with a uniform 2 mm spacer for border moulding and wash impression. Add a handle anteriorly and three stop pads. Send to clinic for the final impression appointment.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast from the final impression using type IV dental stone. This cast forms the working model for all subsequent denture fabrication steps.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Denture Base
steps.description: Fabricate the acrylic base plate over the secondary cast with accurate peripheral extension and stable seating. The base plate carries the occlusal rims to the clinic for the jaw relation record appointment.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Occlusal Rims
steps.description: Attach wax rims to the base plate at correct height (upper: ~22 mm anterior, ~18 mm posterior; lower: proportional). Doctor uses these rims to record jaw relations, midline, smile line, and canine position at the next appointment.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: CD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange Acryrock acrylic teeth on the articulated casts following prescribed shade, mould, midline, and occlusal scheme. Send the wax trial denture to the clinic for patient try-in before processing is approved.

steps.name: CD Processing
steps.description: Flask the approved wax trial denture and process in heat-cured acrylic (conventional pack or injection). Deflask after bench cooling, remove all flash, re-check occlusion on the articulator, and polish to a high shine before delivery.

steps.name: CD Special Tray
steps.description: Fabricate a custom acrylic special tray over the primary cast with a uniform 2 mm spacer for border moulding and wash impression. Add a handle anteriorly and three stop pads. Send to clinic for the final impression appointment.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast from the final impression using type IV dental stone. This cast forms the working model for all subsequent denture fabrication steps.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Denture Base
steps.description: Fabricate the acrylic base plate over the secondary cast with accurate peripheral extension and stable seating. The base plate carries the occlusal rims to the clinic for the jaw relation record appointment.

steps.name: CD Occlusal Rims – Occlusal Rims
steps.description: Attach wax rims to the base plate at correct height (upper: ~22 mm anterior, ~18 mm posterior; lower: proportional). Doctor uses these rims to record jaw relations, midline, smile line, and canine position at the next appointment.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: BPS Teeth Setting
steps.description: Arrange Ivoclar SR Vivodent BPS teeth following the BPS protocol with programmed condylar guidance settings from the articulator. Verify esthetics and occlusion before sending the trial to the clinic for the BPS try-in appointment.

steps.name: BPS CD Processing
steps.description: Process the BPS denture using the SR-Ivocap injection technique (Ivoclar) to minimise processing shrinkage and ensure superior fit. Follow the prescribed injection cycle, deflask, and polish to final finish before delivery.

steps.name: Relining / Rebasing / Repair
steps.description: For relining: add new acrylic to the tissue-bearing surface of the existing denture. For rebasing: replace the full denture base while retaining existing teeth. For repair: rejoin fractured sections with fresh acrylic. Verify fit and occlusion on the working cast after each procedure before delivery.

steps.name: Teeth Setting Correction
steps.description: Reset or reposition the teeth on the trial denture per the doctor's notes from the try-in. Re-articulate if needed. Send the corrected wax trial back to the clinic for a second try-in before processing.

steps.name: TPD Special Tray (Distal Extension cases)
steps.description: Fabricate a custom special tray for distal extension cases designed for the altered cast impression technique. Keep the free-end saddle area open and fully extended to capture functional tissue displacement accurately.

steps.name: TPD Denture Base
steps.description: Fabricate the acrylic base plate over the secondary cast for the transitional partial denture. Mark all extension boundaries clearly based on the impression borders.

steps.name: TPD Occlusal Rims
steps.description: Construct wax occlusal rims on the acrylic base plate at the appropriate height for the partially edentulous arch. Send to clinic for bite registration at the jaw relation appointment.

steps.name: Articulation
steps.description: Mount the upper and lower working casts on the articulator using the face-bow transfer and bite registration provided by the doctor. Verify centric relation occlusion before setting teeth or processing.

steps.name: TPD Teeth Setting
steps.description: Set acrylic teeth on the articulated TPD at the prescribed positions. Verify occlusion and midline. Send the wax trial to the clinic for patient try-in before processing.

steps.name: TPD Processing
steps.description: Flask and process the approved TPD trial in heat-cured acrylic resin. Deflask, remove flash, trim connector areas, and polish to a smooth finish. Verify fit and occlusion on the cast before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour a secondary working cast from the impression using type IV dental stone. Allow complete setting, trim and separate dies, and verify finish line accuracy before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Onlay Fabrication
steps.description: Cast the metal onlay from the wax pattern. Verify all box-form coverage and marginal accuracy under magnification. Check cusp contacts and proximal form before polishing and delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour a secondary working cast from the impression using type IV dental stone. Allow complete setting, trim and separate dies, and verify finish line accuracy before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Onlay Fabrication
steps.description: Press or mill the ceramic onlay to the die. Check internal fit, proximal contacts, and cusp coverage under magnification. Characterize, glaze, and crystallize to match shade before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour a secondary working cast from the impression using type IV dental stone. Allow complete setting, trim and separate dies, and verify finish line accuracy before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Inlay Fabrication
steps.description: Cast the metal inlay and verify precise box-form seating on the die. Check proximal contacts and marginal integrity under magnification. Polish before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour a secondary working cast from the impression using type IV dental stone. Allow complete setting, trim and separate dies, and verify finish line accuracy before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: Inlay Fabrication
steps.description: Press or mill the ceramic inlay to the die. Verify internal fit, interproximal contacts, and occlusal clearance. Characterize, glaze, and crystallize to shade before delivery.

steps.name: Post and Core Fabrication
steps.description: Wax the post to fit the prepared root canal and build the core to the prescribed height and shape. Cast in the appropriate alloy. Verify post length, core build-up dimensions, and anti-rotation feature on the model before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Crown Fabrication
steps.description: Cast the all-metal crown from the wax pattern. Verify marginal fit on the die, check occlusal thickness and proximal contacts. Polish to a high luster before delivery.

steps.name: Secondary Cast
steps.description: Pour the secondary working cast using type IV dental stone. Separate and trim individual dies under magnification. Mark the finish line clearly before any wax pattern or milling work begins.

steps.name: FVC Wax Pattern
steps.description: Carve the crown or coping wax pattern on the die to correct anatomical contours. Verify marginal adaptation, occlusal clearance (1.5 mm minimum), and axial profile before investing for casting.

steps.name: Metal Coping
steps.description: Cast the metal coping, verify marginal fit on the die, and prepare the metal surface (sandblast, oxidise, apply opaque) for ceramic bonding. Ceramic build-up proceeds after the coping is approved by the doctor.

steps.name: Bisque Trial
steps.description: Send the bisque-baked (pre-glaze) restoration to the clinic for try-in. Doctor to evaluate shade match, surface contour, marginal fit, and occlusion. Return with written instructions before final glaze firing and delivery.
